question.text: An option has theta of -0.05. If the option is currently priced at Rs. 2.75, what will be the approximate price tomorrow (all else equal)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Theta = -0.05 means the option loses Rs. 0.05 per day. New price = 2.75 + (-0.05) = Rs. 2.70. **The Trap:** Theta is negative for long positions — the premium DECREASES each day. **Cross-Exam Context:** Theta/time decay calculation is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Time decay accelerates as expiry approaches. The last few days see the steepest drop in time value.
